series_items.subject: [PATCH v6 1/2] mm: track DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new counter to track the number of dirty pages with the dropbehind flag set, which is used by RWF_DONTCACHE writes. The counter, WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY, is incremented when a page is dirtied via RWF_DONTCACHE and decremented when it's cleaned or cleared. This allows the writeback flusher to expedite writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE writes without flushing the entire BDI's dirty pages. The patch modifies several kernel functions to update this counter correctly, ensuring accurate tracking of these special dirty pages.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that folio_test_clear_dropbehind() can be called concurrently by multiple lookups, and suggested adding a lock to prevent double-decrementing of WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY counter.
review_comments.raw_body:
Yeah, good catch with the folio_test_clear_dropbehind(). Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

series_items.subject: [PATCH v6 2/2] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's writeback mechanism to improve performance in the IOCB_DONTCACHE path. It introduces a background flusher that writes back dirty pages, reducing lock contention and latency. The patch also adds targeted dirty tracking using a dedicated WB_start_dontcache bit and per-wb counters to limit the scope of expedited writeback. This approach helps preserve I/O batching while improving performance in high-latency scenarios.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is generally good and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by addressing several bugs and optimizing the handling of dirty pages. The changes include tracking DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kicking the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and adding a mechanism to run 'perf lock' around testcases. Additionally, the patch series includes several performance optimizations, such as using atomic operations and moving function calls outside locked sections to prevent lost wakeups and spin_unlock_irq() re-enabling interrupts while holding the i_pages xa_lock.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that __filemap_get_folio_mpol() uses atomic folio_test_clear_dropbehind(), but the patch description does not mention any changes to this function, and requested a clarification on whether this change is intended
review_comments.raw_body:
Yeah, good catch with the folio_test_clear_dropbehind(). Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza
